cityDNA.coreFantasy: A source-limited Aboriginal songline desert city where a caravanserai beacon, covered bazaar, sun-disc registry court, and first-path songstone suggest route law, memory, trade, and desert orientation.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Only one hero image is available, so Warramirra should be treated as a provisional v2 analysis rather than a fully locked district file.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: From the available hero source, Warramirra appears to turn red-rock routes, songline memory, caravan reception, market shade, and sun-disc law into a desert civic identity.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Red rock desert, sandstone streets, linen shade, caravan approaches, covered bazaar roofs, blue caravanserai signal light, sun-disc registry forms, and distant songline stone formations.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: Aboriginal-inspired route-memory culture with desert hospitality, songline orientation, and market exchange visible from the hero source.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Songline stone, first path, sun discs, caravanserai beacon, covered bazaar, red rock, and road memory symbolize orientation through story and law.

canonLocks: Warramirra has only one uploaded hero source in this batch: WMR-HERO-NW-v001 and its sidecar.
The Wonder is Aboriginal Songline Stone of the First Path.
The Seat of Power is Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court.
The only hero-locked secondary major landmark is Covered Bazaar in visible Section 2.
No WMR street images or street sidecars are present, so exact district civic halls, monuments, and minor landmark nooks are not locked from this upload.
provisionalCanon: Warramirra likely includes Transit / Caravan / Rail and Grand Market functions, but exact street anchors are source-limited until street packages exist.
rejectedReads: generic desert market without songline memory
cautionFlags: Source-limited file: do not invent WMR S1-S5 street anchors.
Keep Aboriginal Songline Stone, Caravanserai Beacon, Sun Disc Registry, and Covered Bazaar legible from the hero source.

civilization.people: The hero source suggests a desert route city with citizens moving through caravan reception and bazaar spaces, but street-scale occupations remain provisional.
civilization.governance: Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court likely governs route law, market access, and public memory; details are source-limited.
civilization.religion: Songline memory and first-path rites appear central from the wonder identity.

streets.appearance: The single hero view shows a red-rock desert city organized around a caravanserai beacon, covered bazaar, sun-disc court, and distant songline stone.
streets.movement: Movement appears to follow caravan approaches, market shade, registry courts, and red-rock routes; street-level routing is not verified.
streets.lighting: Harsh sun, red-rock reflection, linen shade, and a blue beacon define the available visual read.
streets.weatherEffects: Heat, dust, and glare make shade and route knowledge central.
streets.dayCycle: Caravan and market functions are visible from the hero source.
streets.nightCycle: Night behavior is not street-verified; retain only beacon and market-lantern inference.

housing.wealthy: Likely shaded court houses near registry and caravan routes; source-limited.
housing.middle: Likely market and road-family homes; source-limited.
housing.poor: Likely service housing near road and market edges; source-limited.
